articles.summary: Sprout Social's 2026 Social Intelligence Report [5] is here to help social marketers change that - and drive growth by tapping into the conversation when it's actually happening.

articles.title: BOT TRAFFIC NOW EXCEEDS TRAFFIC FROM HUMAN USERS
articles.summary: Bot traffic has surpassed human traffic online for the first time. It accounts for about 57% of web page requests on Cloudflare's network. Agent and agentic browser traffic is up nearly 8,000% in 2025. Automated traffic is growing about 8X faster than human traffic. AI agents are now a primary audience layer. Measurement now requires citation rates, AI share of voice, and referral traffic from AI platforms.

articles.title: THERE'S A BIG DIFFERENCE BETWEEN USING AI AND BEING AI-FIRST
articles.summary: Marketing teams are becoming leaner, more generalist, and more like creative studios than production pipelines. AI handles execution while humans focus on strategy, narrative, and taste. Teams are moving beyond using AI to speed up workflows toward rethinking systems. Differentiation comes from doing less and prioritizing curated signals over volume. AI search is reshaping discovery through structured data, proof, and cross-channel presence.

articles.title: THE PRINCIPLES OF BUYABILITY: WHY STRONG DEALS STALL AND WHAT SEPARATES THE VENDORS WHO GET CHOSEN
articles.summary: B2B deals are more likely to close when the purchase feels safe and defensible, not just superior. About 40% of deals stall because buying groups cannot agree, and 81% go to vendors already familiar to most stakeholders. Finance, legal, and procurement teams control roughly 50% of decision influence, making cross-functional trust critical. Buyers are 3X more likely to choose a heavily recommended vendor than one with a better product or lower price.

articles.title: I BEGGED THIS EMAIL EXPERT FOR HIS SECRETS (59 MINUTE VIDEO)
articles.summary: Daniel Bustamante explains how a dormant 50,000-person email list generated $150K in a 7-day launch using a simple three-email weekly system. The cadence includes a Monday curation email, a Wednesday deep dive, and a Friday story or case study to build trust. He covers how email and social work together, structuring lead magnets, and balancing nurture with sales in welcome flows. He also shows how AI tools like Claude help streamline launch campaigns.

articles.title: INSIDE THE LINKEDIN CONTENT SYSTEM I BUILT IN A WEEKEND
articles.summary: A creator built a local LinkedIn content system in a weekend that replaces multiple SaaS tools and cuts daily workflow from 3 hours to about 20 minutes. The tool uses engagement data to surface trending topics, track content pillars, suggest reposts, and identify audience signals for outreach. It also generates adjacent content angles from past high performers and ensures balanced coverage across themes. By replacing feed scrolling with signal-based inputs, it streamlines ideation and distribution.

articles.title: YOUR MESSAGE REACHES TECH PROFESSIONALS IN A TRUSTED EDITORIAL CONTEXT
articles.summary: When professionals open TLDR, they're seeking news and insights that are genuinely relevant to their work in tech. Our 7 million subscribers include developers, product managers, marketers, and

articles.title: founders reading with real interest and engagement. Sponsor TLDR
articles.summary: and connect with an audience that's actively paying attention.

articles.title: AHREFS AEO COURSE (ONLINE COURSE)
articles.summary: This free course explains how AI search engines decide what to cite and recommend. It also covers competitor gap analysis and prompt research for AI discovery. It shows how to structure content for citation and build brand mentions across the web. The course has 12 lessons and takes about an hour and 26 minutes to complete.

articles.title: IF I WERE STARTING FROM ZERO TODAY, I'D BUILD MY AI BRAIN ON GITHUB FIRST
articles.summary: A GitHub repository acts as a living brain, using markdown files to store priorities and decision logs. A background file holds key context while a journal file forces regular prioritization. A Claude Code routine runs in the cloud each night, scanning Slack, email, calendar, and meeting transcripts. It then drafts replies, updates tasks, surfaces summaries, and writes changes back to GitHub. Notion is used for drafting and editing, Slack is used for notifications through a custom bot, and the whole setup runs continuously without manual prompts.

articles.title: BONUS, FULLY REMOTE)
articles.summary: We're hiring a senior PMM to own product marketing at TLDR. You'll define our positioning, build out sales enablement, and lead every launch. Learn more [17].

articles.title: FEEDBACK LOOPS REQUIRE REAL FEEDBACK. AI DRIVES IT UNDERGROUND
articles.summary: Feedback loops rely on real critique and experimentation. These systems break when feedback is unstructured or detached from decisions. AI increases noise by producing abundant low-value feedback at scale. Teams also move away from direct cross-functional conversations. As speed becomes the priority, learning slows and signals get suppressed. Without strong feedback systems, companies drift and corrections become slower and more expensive.

articles.title: WHY TIKTOK IS EXPANDING ITS PREMIUM ADS PUSH AND WHAT THAT MEANS FOR YOU
articles.summary: TikTok's 2026 NewFronts framed the platform as a sequential storytelling environment that mirrors television with new ad formats. Prime Time lets brands serve up to three ads within a 15-minute high-engagement window, while TopReach bundles the first app-open and in-feed placement to capture attention during cultural moments. With only 26% of marketers using TikTok campaigns, the platform argues for greater budget reallocation from linear and streaming TV.

articles.title: ACKNOWLEDGEMENT IS THE CX STRATEGY MOST ORGANIZATIONS ARE OVERLOOKING
articles.summary: Negativity bias makes difficult customer experiences more memorable than positive ones. Unacknowledged friction therefore shapes long-term brand perception. Acknowledging complexity, even without fixing it, reduces resistance and builds psychological safety. Validation changes how friction is remembered by the customer. Embedding this across onboarding, digital flows, and automated communications turns acknowledgement into a journey-wide loyalty strategy rather than a support tactic.

articles.title: META CONTINUES TO SCREW OVER ITS CREATORS
articles.summary: Meta creators report 60% to 90% revenue drops since April as AI content gets prioritized and new creators are paid flat rates.

articles.title: POINTS TO REMEMBER WHEN USING FIFA IP IN MARKETING MATERIALS
articles.summary: FIFA restricts use of “World Cup”, “FIFA”, “FIFA World Cup 26”, logos, slogans, mascots, and the trophy in marketing, so use generic terms unless licensed.

articles.title: GEN Z'S LATEST CAREER FLEX: A BOARDROOM SEAT
articles.summary: Gen Z is securing board seats at companies, trading experience and mentorship for influence and insight into younger consumers that older boards often miss.
